title: Blueprints for Morning
content:
Morning lays its blueprints on the kitchen table—
steam, light, something forgiving in the air.
I promise the day I will try to be soft where I was sharp,
present where I was missing, honest even in the small ways.

It is a plain miracle to be ordinary.
To button a shirt without hurrying your own heart.
To eat an apple and taste every step the tree made toward you.
I draw a breath. I sign my name at the bottom of it.

title: The Quiet Between Messages
content:
We live in the quiet between messages—
read receipts, unfinished drafts, a hello left hovering.
I want to learn the economy of gentleness:
to spend less apology and more presence,
less lightning, more lamp.

Come sit with me in the middle of our day.
Let our thumbs rest. Let our voices remember
that patience is a kind of touch you can hear.

title: Inventory
content:
I kept an inventory of what I failed to be.
It grew like a city with no parks.
One afternoon I left the clipboard on a bench
and listened to a fountain tell its whole life to the air.

What if I am not a problem to solve
but a neighborhood to walk—slowly, curiously—
turning corners into surprises,
letting small kindnesses plant their trees.

title: Late Shift
content:
The screen glows like a moon that isn't done talking.
You practice the ancient art of making meaning
out of pages that try to hide it.
If no one told you yet: your effort is a kind of prayer.

Take three minutes to learn the shape of your breath.
This is also study. This is also progress.
You do not have to finish the whole night.
Just finish this minute with mercy.

title: Dishwater Epiphany
content:
In the dishwater I find a galaxy—
small planets of citrus, a comet of foam.
I am so tired of trying to be remarkable.
Let the ordinary astonish me instead.

I rinse a glass until it remembers light.
I stack the bowls like quiet moons.
The evening forgives me for being simple.
I forgive the evening for being brief.

title: The Long Rain
content:
It isn't the storm but the long rain after—
the slow insistence, the careful wearing down.
I want a solution with teeth, but what I get
is a mop, a towel, a neighbor with a joke.

We do not conquer the flood. We accompany the water
until it remembers the river.
If all you did today was keep the lamp dry,
that is still a kind of rescue.

title: Light Maintenance
content:
Hope is not a mood; it is maintenance.
Tighten the screws on the window of your attention.
Oil the hinge of your gratitude until it swings.
Replace the bulb over your doubt with a warmer watt.

You are not naive for trying again.
You are an electrician of your own small dawns.

title: The Table for Two
content:
We are not a miracle. We are a practice—
a table set for two in a house of weather.
Tonight I bring you the day I carried;
you bring me the part of it that loved you back.

Between us: bread, a story, the unafraid truth.
Under us: floorboards learning our weight by heart.
Around us: the long hallway of everything we haven't said
lighting one lamp at a time.

title: Receipt
content:
The heart prints a receipt—
items returned: plans, keys, Sunday afternoons.
No refunds on time. Store credit in lessons.

I keep the paper until it softens in my pocket.
I read it on trains, in waiting rooms, at red lights,
not to hurt myself—just to remember
that I once bought a future and meant it.

title: Salt
content:
There is salt in every good story—
the part where the voice breaks, the part where
we stop pretending we weren't afraid.
Salt keeps the memory honest; it keeps the body real.

Cry if you must. Then drink a glass of water.
Let the ocean you are keep its tides.

title: Staying
content:
I thought love was fireworks. Sometimes it's a porch light
left on because they said they'd be late.
Sometimes it's a ride to the airport at 4 a.m.,
or the last orange saved for you.

Staying isn't spectacle. It's choreography—
a thousand small steps toward the same room.

title: The Net
content:
We built a net out of Tuesdays—
coffee, a meme, a question you really meant.
When I fell, it wasn't the text that caught me
but the years of string we had tied without hurry.

May we keep weaving the ordinary.
May we be foolish enough to believe
that a small net can hold a big sky.

title: Return Policy
content:
I returned the anger for store credit
and bought a gentler sentence:
I did my best with what I knew.
Next time I'll know more.

The clerk nodded as if this happens hourly.
I left the shop lighter, as if regret
were a coat you could learn to wear open.

title: Syllabus for Joy
content:
Week 1: Recess. Week 2: Recess.
Final: A nap you don't apologize for.
Extra credit: Share your snacks with a future self.

Attendance policy: Please arrive exactly as you are.
Late work accepted, especially if it comes
with a story about the sky.

title: Spare Room
content:
I cleaned out a spare room in my chest—
boxed the rage, labeled the sadness, opened a window.
When tenderness knocked, I had somewhere to seat it.
We watched the afternoon learn our names.

Healing is housework, mostly.
The party comes later.

title: Terms and Conditions
content:
I agree to hold your hand under fluorescent lighting,
not just at sunset. I agree to listen
when I'm sure I'm right, and to be surprised.
I agree to leave space for your silences
without packing them with my fear.

You agree to name what hurts before it hardens.
We both agree to eat before arguing.
We initial here: forgiveness in pencil,
because we plan to rewrite it often.

title: Casserole Theology
content:
At the wake someone brings a casserole—
macaroni arguing tenderly with cheese.
Loss is an architecture. Community is the roof.
We do not keep out the rain,
we make a place to stay dry together.

Bless the hands that carry heat
into a room that forgot it could be warm.

title: The Unsent Playlist
content:
I made you a playlist I will never send.
Track one: the day we nearly said the same thing.
Track two: the train we missed and the hour it gave us.
Final track: the question I hum in lowercase.

Not all music is for sharing.
Some of it is just to remind the body
that rhythm was a language before words.

title: Spare Keys
content:
I keep your spare key in the bowl by the door—
not to use it, just to be honest.
Some days I move it to a drawer,
then back again, like weather.

Healing is a choreography of drawers and doors.
One day the key will quietly choose the bin.
I won't throw it away. It will throw me forward.

title: Field Notes on Tears
content:
Day 12: tears during the third commercial.
Day 19: tears at the crosswalk for no reason except traffic.
Conclusion: the body knows when the inbox is full.
Recommendation: archive by water.

Final note: every time I cry, I see farther.
The horizon is a soft instrument.
